findings.title: getCaseHistory reads all history files sequentially
findings.issue: getCaseHistory reads and JSON-parses every history file to find one eval case. With hundreds of history files, this becomes O(n) disk reads per request.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable for current scale (10s-100s of files). If history grows large, consider an index file or caching. Not blocking.
findings.impact: Slow response on skills with very large history (500+ runs). Unlikely in practice.

findings.title: History compare regression direction naming
findings.issue: computeRegressions(runB, runA) passes B as 'current' and A as 'previous'. The naming convention (A=older, B=newer) is consistent but the parameter order reversal could confuse future maintainers.
findings.suggestion: Add a comment clarifying the parameter order. Not blocking.
findings.impact: Minor confusion for future developers reading the code.

notes: All 8 user stories implemented: enriched data model, baseline runs, history filtering, cross-session comparison, trend visualization, rerun capabilities, API client extensions, per-case history. 959 tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly. All endpoints validate input and handle errors. No security issues (local-only eval server). Backward compatibility maintained via optional fields.
